sub_sections.title: Project's pathways towards impact
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO delivers impact through five pathways (PATH-EI-01 to PATH-EI-05) addressing all call expected impacts (EI-01 to EI-05) and both expected outcomes (EO-01, EO-02). PATH-EI-01 implements the European Apply AI Strategy via three TRL-5 demonstrators: OBJ-4 (Healthcare demonstrator — clinical decision support) targets diagnostic accuracy within 10% of specialist clinician performance on 500 de-identified clinical cases spanning 5 diagnostic domains via D5-02 (Healthcare demonstrator evaluation dataset, M42); OBJ-5 (Manufacturing demonstrator — process optimisation) targets ≥15% defect reduction AND ≥10% energy savings at ISIA over a 3-month operational period via D6-02 (Manufacturing process optimisation demonstrator software, M42); OBJ-6 (Logistics transfer demonstrator — cross-sector generalisation) targets ≥20% disruption recovery improvement AND ≥15% delivery schedule adherence improvement across 3 supply chain corridors via D7-02 (Cross-sector generalisation evaluation dataset, M42) with architectural transferability mapping to in-vehicle autonomous driving. Core research underpins all demonstrators: OBJ-1 (Neuro-symbolic planning engine for autonomous task decomposition) achieves ≥40% plan success improvement over ReAct and Reflexion on WebArena, SWE-bench, and GAIA for tasks requiring >10 sequential steps; OBJ-2 (Unified adaptive memory architecture) achieves ≥30% improvement in task coherence and factual consistency over context-window-only baselines in sequences exceeding 50 agent steps; OBJ-3 (Decentralised multi-agent coordination protocols) coordinates ≥5 heterogeneous agents with ≥25% joint task completion improvement over independent agent baselines AND formal proof of protocol convergence; OBJ-8 (External Tool and API Orchestration Layer) delivers a typed registry supporting ≥30 external tools/APIs with ≥95% invocation success rate, ≤5s mean recovery time, AND 100% provenance traces. PATH-EI-02 drives EDIH-mediated deployment: ≥3 hospital networks in post-project adoption (KPI-01), ≥5 manufacturing SMEs via ≥2 EDIHs, and ≥10 European SMEs/startups building on D9-02 (Open-source software release, M36) by M48 (KPI-04). PATH-EI-03 advances European AI science: ≥20 top-venue publications (KPI-06), ≥100 citations within 3 years (KPI-09), and benchmark adoption by ≥5 external research groups (KPI-07). PATH-EI-04 strengthens EU strategic autonomy through D4-01 (Multi-agent coordination protocol specification, M18) enabling ≥1 standardisation submission to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE (KPI-08). PATH-EI-05 unlocks economic potential via OBJ-7 (Open framework release and ecosystem contribution) targeting ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months of release, AI-on-demand platform contributions, ≥2 TEF validations, AND ≥3 EDIH transfers, with statistically significant SOTA improvement (p<0.05) on ≥3 task categories (KPI-11). Target groups: hospital clinical informatics teams, manufacturing SMEs, logistics operators, AI researchers, European startups, and standardisation bodies. Barriers: regulatory timelines for clinical AI (mitigated by WP5 EU AI Act compliance evidence), data access constraints (mitigated by ISIA and ELI operational environments), and open-source community formation (mitigated by BAL community management under WP9).

sub_sections.title: Measures to maximise impact - Dissemination, exploitation and communication
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO's dissemination, exploitation, and communication plan maximises impact across all five pathways. Dissemination: ATU, BIIS, CERIA, and FIIT produce ≥20 peer-reviewed publications in NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AAMAS, and AAAI (M12–M60), deposited under CC-BY open access within six months of acceptance. BAL releases D9-02 (Open-source software release, M36) on GitHub and the AI-on-demand platform under Apache 2.0 licence. FIIT publishes D8-02 (MAESTRO benchmark suite dataset, M42) as reproducible evaluation infrastructure targeting ≥5 external research groups. NIHS disseminates clinical validation results through EDIH-facilitated engagement with ≥3 hospital networks by M48. ISIA transfers manufacturing AI capabilities to ≥5 SMEs via ≥2 EDIHs and EIT Manufacturing network. D1-02 (Data management plan, M6) ensures FAIR compliance for all research outputs, with sensitive clinical data anonymised per GDPR before release. Exploitation: NIHS develops post-project adoption agreements with hospital networks grounded in D5-02 (Healthcare demonstrator evaluation dataset, M42) and EU AI Act high-risk compliance evidence. ISIA establishes consulting and licensing revenue from D6-02 (Manufacturing process optimisation demonstrator software, M42) technology transfer. ELI produces a commercial integration roadmap for MAESTRO capabilities across ≥3 logistics corridors. BAL manages an open-core IPR strategy: core framework, benchmark suite, and protocol specifications under Apache 2.0; sector-specific configurations licensable by responsible partners (NIHS, ISIA, ELI). CERIA targets D4-01 (Multi-agent coordination protocol specification, M18) adoption by ≥3 external multi-agent projects and ≥1 submission to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE, accompanied by an EU AI Act multi-agent transparency alignment report. Communication: BAL leads public engagement under WP9 T9-03 including project website, social media, and stakeholder events targeting the AI research community, industrial adopters, and policy makers. Policy feedback is generated through CERIA's standardisation activities and the EU AI Act alignment report. Sustainability is ensured through three reinforcing mechanisms: self-sustaining open-source community maintenance of the MAESTRO framework, operational embedding in NIHS, ISIA, and ELI commercial environments creating post-project maintenance obligations, and normative embedding through CERIA's standardisation submissions establishing MAESTRO protocols as international references.

sub_sections.title: Summary
sub_sections.content: Impact Canvas — Specific Needs: European AI agent technology lacks formal verifiability, long-horizon coherence, and auditable multi-agent coordination, limiting trustworthy deployment in safety-critical sectors. Expected Results: D2-02 (Planning engine software prototype, M36), D3-02 (Adaptive memory system software, M30), D4-01 (Multi-agent coordination protocol specification, M18), D4-02 (Decentralised coordination software, M36), D8-01 (Evaluation framework and benchmark specification, M12), three TRL-5 demonstrators: D5-02 (Healthcare demonstrator evaluation dataset, M42), D6-02 (Manufacturing process optimisation demonstrator software, M42), D7-02 (Cross-sector generalisation evaluation dataset, M42); plus D8-02 (MAESTRO benchmark suite dataset, M42) and D9-02 (Open-source software release, M36). DEC Measures: ≥20 top-venue publications under CC-BY, Apache 2.0 open-source release, EDIH technology transfer, AI-on-demand platform contribution, ISO/IEC standardisation submission. Target Groups: hospital networks, manufacturing SMEs, logistics operators, AI researchers, European startups, standardisation bodies. Outcomes: clinical AI adoption pathway (≥3 hospital networks), manufacturing technology transfer (≥5 SMEs via ≥2 EDIHs), logistics integration roadmap, benchmark standard (≥5 research groups), open-source ecosystem (≥500 GitHub stars). Impacts: Apply AI Strategy across healthcare, manufacturing, logistics (EI-01); EDIH-mediated industrial uptake (EI-02); European AI research infrastructure (EI-03); EU strategic autonomy (EI-04); economic potential of AI agents unlocked (EI-05).
